svg.text: Binary Search Pattern — Templates and Variants
• Exact match: classic [lo, hi], return mid on equality. Use when value is guaranteed.
• lower_bound / upper_bound: find first index where predicate flips false→true.
Half-open [lo, hi); shrink hi = mid on true, lo = mid + 1 on false. Returns lo at end.
• Parametric search: binary-search the answer. Predicate p(x) checks feasibility.
Used for "min capacity to ship in D days", "Koko bananas", "split array largest sum".
Pre-condition: predicate must be monotone — once true (or false), stays true (or false).
If you have monotonicity, you have binary search. The hard part is proving monotonicity.
if predicate(mid): hi = mid # mid might be the answer
return lo # first index where predicate is true
One template handles 90% of binary-search problems. Memorise it.
